svg.text: Target: SME compliance manager. Goal: understand gap, know exactly what to do, track progress, get help.
Battery passport not implemented. Required for portable batteries above 2kWh from 18 Feb 2027.
ProScan 3000 has a 3.2kWh battery — exceeds the Article 13 threshold.
battery_capacity_wh (3.2kWh) > 2kWh threshold (Article 13) · market includes EU · battery_type = portable
Euro 100,000+ fine per EU market · Market ban with no grace period · Marketplace delisting
Brand damage and loss of EU distribution partnerships.
Supply chain data collection takes 6–9 months — start now.
Confirm battery specs: type, capacity (Wh), cell chemistry
Collect carbon footprint data per kWh across battery lifecycle
Collect recycled content data: cobalt, lithium, nickel, lead %
Complete supply chain due diligence documentation
Register product in EPREL (battery product group)
Generate Digital Product Passport with QR code
Attach QR code to product and packaging before EU market placement
Update technical file and Declaration of Conformity
Your ProScan 3000 has a 3.2kWh portable battery, exceeding the 2kWh threshold in Article 13 of Regulation (EU) 2023/1542.
From 18 February 2027, a Digital Battery Passport — a QR-linked record with carbon footprint, cell chemistry,
recycled content, and supply chain data — is mandatory before the product can be placed on the EU market.
Compliance assistant — pre-loaded with this gap and product context
What if I miss the deadline?
Hi — I have full context for this gap. Ask me anything.
Ask about steps, deadlines, consequences, or other products…
